landmark_label: SW distal grate across Bay (NAVD88 3.52)
observed_qualitative: water in grate throat 9.25 in below SW grate top; light rain falling, minor rain water on street (user's false-alarm check during active Flood Watch)
weather_in_window: light rain; Flood Watch active; wind S 3.3 kt (offshore sector)
notes: Mid-RISING tide spot-check (~1h before the 15:30 peak; obs time 14:28 (user-confirmed); gauge reading is 14:24, 4 min prior on a rising tide — effectively concurrent). Concurrent gauge 5.71 MLLW = 2.888 NAVD88; measured local 2.749 NAVD88 -> local minus gauge = -0.139 ft. MATCHES the v0.8 offshore-wind adjustment (-0.13) almost exactly, but at (a) the LOWEST SH level yet tested (5.71 vs the 6.17-7.29 calibration range), (b) mid-tide not peak, and (c) near-calm wind (S 3.3 kt): suggests the offset rides on direction/setup rather than wind SPEED. Grate-throat reading per the established 5/31 protocol (below grate tops this is drain-system water connected via the outfall).

landmark_label: Storm grate at user's corner (NE)
observed_qualitative: water 2.25 in above NE grate; violent burst beginning
weather_in_window: torrential convective burst on high tide; Flood Watch + expired FF Warning
notes: EVENT #4 - 2026-07-09 compound flash flood, burst ON the 15:30 high tide. Measured street peak 5.08 NAVD88 (+18.7 in vs SW grate) at 15:56; between 7/6 (+15.4) and Oct 30 (+21) by street numbers; user judged it as-bad-or-worse than Oct 30, water to the house FOUNDATION at peak (walkway side). SH gauge MALFUNCTIONED during the cell (spikes to 11.87 MLLW; Battery flat - instrument, not water); true bay base ~6.0 MLLW ~3.2 NAVD88 interpolated across the garbage -> drains ~90 pct blocked; rain lift ~22 in from base. Rise rate NE grate +7.75 in / 12 min (fastest recorded). See assets/observations/2026-07-09/.

landmark_label: Storm grate at user's corner (NE)
observed_qualitative: water in throat 2.5 in below NE grate top (dry above)
weather_in_window: clear evening tide; offshore SSW ~13 kt
notes: 2026-07-13 street-regime tide event, gauge peak 6.695 MLLW @19:48. Peak-window enhancement: SE -0.108 / SW -0.147 / upstream -0.068 ft vs concurrent gauge (mean ~-0.11) with SSW 13 kt = offshore sector -> FIFTH consecutive offshore event near the v0.8 -0.13 adjustment, this time at classic moderate wind speed. NE grate stayed dry (elev 3.80 > water) as predicted. Upstream-grate oscillation (+/-0.5 in 'breathing') = tide pulses through the outfall, first time noted in writing.
